pi_items.source: setup_task.sh:61 and export_result.sh:34 both use 'mysolar' as the app identifier in database queries and API calls to getconfig.json. Confirmed as valid Emoncms app name via OpenEnergyMonitor documentation.
setup_task.sh:44 creates feed with name 'solar_power' via create_feed_if_missing function. export_result.sh:66 retrieves this feed ID as reference under key 'solar_power'.
setup_task.sh:45 creates feed with name 'house_power' via create_feed_if_missing function. export_result.sh:67 retrieves this feed ID as reference under key 'house_power'.
setup_task.sh:46 creates feed with name 'solar_energy_kwh' via create_feed_if_missing function. export_result.sh:68 retrieves this feed ID as reference under key 'solar_kwh'.
setup_task.sh:47 creates feed with name 'house_energy_kwh' via create_feed_if_missing function. export_result.sh:69 retrieves this feed ID as reference under key 'house_kwh'.

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetically generated via Python seed data script
pi_items.source: setup_emoncms.sh:202 begins 'Creating energy monitoring seed data' section. Lines 202-421 contain Python code that generates 30 days of historical power consumption and solar generation data with realistic daily curves and random variations.
setup_task.sh:29 creates feeds with datatype=1 (realtime) and engine=5 (PHPFina) with 10-second interval. Standard Emoncms time-series storage engine.
setup_task.sh:29 creates feeds with options JSON containing interval=10 seconds via curl API call to feed/create.json
setup_task.sh:44,46 create both solar_power and solar_energy_kwh feeds with tag='Solar'
setup_task.sh:45,47 create both house_power and house_energy_kwh feeds with tag='House'
setup_task.sh:29 creates feeds with unit=W in the API call for power feeds

pi_items.verified_value: Emoncms - open-source web application for energy monitoring and time-series data visualization, part of OpenEnergyMonitor project

pi_items.verified_value: Dashboard app for solar PV self-consumption monitoring and visualization
pi_items.source: https://github.com/emoncms/app - MySolar is a standard app in Emoncms that displays solar generation vs. consumption in real-time and historical views

privileged_info_summary: The task involves configuring the MySolar dashboard application in Emoncms to visualize solar PV system performance. The system has four pre-created feeds: solar_power (solar generation power in watts), house_power (house consumption power in watts), solar_energy_kwh (cumulative solar energy in kWh), and house_energy_kwh (cumulative house energy in kWh). These feeds are synthetically generated with 30 days of realistic daily power curves at 10-second intervals using a Python seed data script. The MySolar app must be configured by assigning the correct feed IDs to the app's configuration fields. Emoncms runs in Docker with MariaDB 10.11 database backend.
